steps.title: Connect method to estimates
steps.content: Barrett and Rayfield's suggestion is that bite-force estimates depend heavily on methodology. The strongest table evidence should show two things together: studies using the same method give similar estimates, while studies using different methods give much different estimates. That pattern points to methodology as the variable affecting the results.

steps.title: Check the table pattern
steps.content: Choice A misstates Meers as the lowest maximum when it is the highest. Choice B reports only one study, so it cannot show a method effect. Choice D focuses on same-method differences, which is not the strongest support. Choice C compares the similar muscular-and-skeletal-modeling estimates with the outlying body-mass-scaling and tooth-bone-analysis estimates. The answer is C.
